Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First recall standard Hire to Retire process It typically includes posting a job ad screening candidates conducting interviews making a hiring decision onboarding new employee adding employee to payroll and closing the case Standard order would be sequential Post Screen Interview Decide Onboard Payroll Close

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 1 has Post Screen Decide Interview Onboard Payroll Close with edges Post Screen Screen Decide Screen Interview Decide Onboard Onboard Payroll Payroll Close There is no edge between Interview and Decide so after Screen both Interview and Decide can happen in any order

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So a candidate could be hired without interviewing or interviewed but no decision made and yet the process still proceeds to onboarding and closing That is an anomaly because in standard process interviews are conducted before making a hiring decision and interview results feed into the decision

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In Model 1 there is no edge from Interview to anything else so Interview can occur after Screen at any time including after Decide Onboard Payroll or Close since no order prevents that

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Model 1 therefore has a mostly correct backbone Post Screen Decide Onboard Payroll Close and it also keeps Screen before Interview but it misses the required dependency from Interview to Decide

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 2 has Post Screen Interview Decide loop onboarding xor payroll Close with edges Post Screen Post Interview Interview Decide Decide loop onboarding loop onboarding xor payroll xor payroll Close

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: loop onboarding means Onboard can be repeated multiple times and xor payroll means either Add to Payroll or skip is selected

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This allows repeated onboarding which does not make sense because onboarding is normally done once It also allows skipping payroll which is anomalous because adding a hired employee to payroll is mandatory

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Post Interview means Interview can happen directly after Post without Screen and Screen has no outgoing edge so screening is disconnected from the decision path

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: That means the process can interview and hire without relying on screening while screening becomes a useless side activity This breaks normal hiring logic where screening should precede and inform interviewing

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus Model 2 has multiple anomalies missing screening dependency disconnected screening repeated onboarding and optional payroll while Model 1 mainly has one major structural anomaly the missing dependency from Interview to Decide

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Therefore Model 1 more closely aligns with a normative version of the process because it preserves more of the expected sequence Post Screen then downstream hiring and completion activities whereas Model 2 distorts several core requirements of process correctness and integrity
